mainEntity.name: How long do I have to report a forklift accident in Utah before it\'s too late to file a claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Utah, you should report the injury within 30 days and file a formal claim within one year. Idaho allows 60 days for notice, but reporting immediately is always safer to prevent insurance disputes.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Utah, you generally have 30 days to notify your employer of a workplace injury, but you have up to one year to file a formal claim with the Labor Commission. In Idaho, the notice requirement is 60 days, and the claim filing deadline is typically one year. Missing these deadlines can result in a permanent loss of benefits, including medical coverage and wage replacement. 

While the "statute of limitations" provides a window, waiting even a week to report a forklift injury can lead the insurance company to argue the injury happened at home or elsewhere.

Important timing factors:
- The date of the actual accident or the date the injury became apparent.
- Whether the employer had "actual knowledge" of the crash (even without a written report).
- Deadlines for appealing a denial, which are much shorter than the initial filing window.

Report the injury in writing today if you haven\'t already. Keep a dated copy of any forms you submit to your manager.

Because deadlines vary by state and injury type, a lawyer can track these dates to ensure you don\'t lose thousands of dollars in benefits due to a technicality.
